title: Zouk Counting Coconuts
description: A fun zouk-style song for kids that teaches them to count while learning about coconuts on Saint Lucia.
lyrics:
[Verse 1]
One coconut fall from de tree [CLAP]
One coconut for you and me.
Two coconuts round and brown [SNAP]
Two coconuts all over town.

[Chorus]
Counting coconuts, one two three
Underneath the Lucian sea!
Zouk music makes us happy and bright,
Counting coconuts morning, noon, and night!

[Verse 2]
Three coconuts in de market stall [STOMP]
Three coconuts, hear the vendors call!
Four coconuts, so high up high [CLAP]
Four coconuts way up in the sky!

[Chorus]
Counting coconuts, one two three
Underneath the Lucian sea!
Zouk music makes us happy and bright,
Counting coconuts morning, noon, and night!

[Verse 3]
Five coconuts, oh what a treat! [SNAP]
Five coconuts, something good to eat!
Six coconuts, give them a shake [STOMP]
Six coconuts, for goodness sake!

[Chorus]
Counting coconuts, one two three
Underneath the Lucian sea!
Zouk music makes us happy and bright,
Counting coconuts morning, noon, and night!

[Verse 4]
Seven coconuts, shiny and new! [CLAP]
Seven coconuts, for me and for you!
Eight coconuts, lined in a row [SNAP]
Eight coconuts, watch them grow!
*Wai!* (Hey!)
thumbnailPrompt: A vibrant and colorful cartoon illustration for a children's song cover. The scene is a sunny beach in Saint Lucia with turquoise water and lush green palm trees. Four smiling children of different ethnicities are dancing in the sand, holding coconuts. One child is playing a steelpan, another a ukulele, and a third is shaking maracas. Coconuts are scattered around on the beach. The sky is bright blue with fluffy white clouds. The words "Zouk Counting Coconuts" are prominently displayed in a playful font. The style should be cheerful and inviting, capturing the energy of Caribbean music and culture.
educationalValue: Teaches children to count from one to eight, introduces them to Caribbean culture and music (zouk), and familiarizes them with the concept of coconuts as a local fruit.
